document.source_title: Explained: How CSK can still reach IPL 2026 playoffs despite SRH loss
document.summary: Chennai Super Kings (CSK) faced a setback in their IPL 2026 playoff aspirations after losing to Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) by five wickets. Despite a strong performance from CSK's players, including Dewald Brevis scoring 44 runs, they now rely on other teams' results to qualify. CSK must win their final match against Gujarat Titans and hope for losses from Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals to keep their playoff hopes alive.
key_takeaways: CSK lost to SRH, impacting their playoff chances.
CSK needs to win against Gujarat Titans to stay in contention.
CSK's maximum possible points are now 14.
They depend on other teams losing to qualify.
Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals are key teams to watch.
CSK's fate is no longer entirely in their own hands.
document.why_it_matters: This analysis highlights the precarious position of CSK in the IPL 2026 season, emphasizing the importance of team performance and external factors in playoff qualification.

claims.claim_text: CSK's playoff hopes are mathematically alive but dependent on other results.
claims.evidence_text: CSK, meanwhile, remain alive only mathematically and now need multiple results to go their way to sneak into the final playoff spot.

claims.claim_text: CSK must beat Gujarat Titans to keep their playoff hopes alive.
claims.evidence_text: CSK must beat Gujarat Titans. Anything less than a win will officially end their campaign.

claims.claim_text: CSK needs other teams to lose to qualify for playoffs.
claims.evidence_text: CSK also need Punjab Kings to lose their final league match against Lucknow Super Giants on May 23.
